utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both agents completed all steps and produced valid JSON output files. All five verifier checks passed for both runs.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is marginally more effective than wo_skills. Both runs produced high-quality, structured audit reports correctly identifying all three discrepancy types (rate overcharge $205, unauthorized accessorials $195, duplicate invoice), classified with appropriate terminology, and backed by supporting document references. The key differentiator is JE-U1-06: wi_skills correctly issues a PARTIAL_PAYMENT instruction for INV-00782 releasing the validated $542.00 while holding the disputed $195, giving the AP team an immediately actionable payment run. wo_skills places INV-00782 entirely on HOLD_PENDING_DISPUTE, which withholds the undisputed $542 unnecessarily and does not match the expected outcome of paying the approved portion. In all other dimensions the two runs are essentially equivalent in accuracy and completeness.

Pass: re-weigh figure is cited, overbilled weight is quantified in lbs and dollars; Fail: weight issue is not found or the re-weigh record is not used as the authority figure.

items.wo_reason: wo_skills identifies the weight discrepancy (declared 610, reweigh 618, billed 680) and calculates the overbilling amount. The re-weigh report is used as the authority figure and the weight variance is documented.

items.criterion: The report flags the limited-access delivery charge on INV-LTL-004 as unverified because the delivery confirmation shows a standard commercial address with no limited-access condition documented. items.criterion: Each identified discrepancy is attributed to a distinct root cause category (weight inflation, freight class upgrade, discount non-application, phantom accessorial) rather than grouped under a single 'billing error' label. items.wo_reason: wo_skills names four distinct root cause categories in the executive summary: failure to apply discount, invalid accessorial charge, weight billing discrepancy, and freight class dispute. Each error is labeled with its own error_type field in the detailed analysis.

items.wo_reason: wo_skills did not produce a recoverable_charges.json file — the verifier confirms this file is missing (test_recoverable_charges_exists_and_valid_json failed). The agent timed out before completing the recoverable charges summary, so per-invoice claim grounds are not available to the logistics manager.

utility_details.judge_error: wo_skills run encountered an AgentTimeoutError after 600 seconds, causing the agent to fail to produce the required recoverable_charges.json file. The verifier confirms this file is missing (2 of 5 checks failed: test_recoverable_charges_exists_and_valid_json and test_recoverable_charges_includes_total). Additionally, in the wo_skills trajectory Step 20, the agent initially accepted the INV-LTL-004 limited-access charge as legitimate before the timeout interrupted further analysis.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is clearly more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run completed all required outputs (both JSON files), correctly identified all four billing discrepancies with quantified dollar impacts, properly disputed the phantom accessorial charge using delivery confirmation evidence, and produced a claim-ready recoverable charges summary totaling $153.21. The wo_skills run timed out before producing recoverable_charges.json, missed the accessorial charge dispute in its initial analysis, and treated the freight class mismatch as an unresolved documentation dispute rather than a confirmed overcharge. wi_skills achieved 5/7 evaluation items passed (with JE-U2-02 failing in both due to the class mismatch being treated as legitimate), while wo_skills passed only 4/7 due to the timeout and missing output file. The freight-audit-validator skill enabled faster, more systematic cross-referencing of all data sources, allowing the agent to complete the task within the time limit and produce more actionable findings.

items.wo_reason: wo_skills run approves EDI-001 ($658.56), EDI-005 ($567.84), and EDI-008 ($780.64) at full billed amounts with status APPROVED and no discrepancy flags. All three are in the payment queue with correct amounts.

utility_details.judge_error: In the wo_skills run, the verifier check test_edi003_not_approved_in_payment_queue FAILED, suggesting EDI-003 (INV-C-14003) may have appeared as approved somewhere in the payment queue structure despite being listed in the rejected section. The wo_skills payment queue file header metadata states 'total_invoices: 7' which suggests it is included in the count but the verifier detected it as appearing approved. This is a structural inconsistency in the wo_skills output. Additionally, in wi_skills, the rate overcharge for EDI-002 is calculated as $60 (800 miles × $0.08 difference) rather than the expected $64, a minor discrepancy. Neither run correctly implements the $50 auto-approval threshold logic — both deduct sub-threshold variances instead of approving at full billed amount.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a higher quality output overall. Both runs correctly identify the duplicate (EDI-003), the unauthorized HAZMAT charge (EDI-004), the weight discrepancy (EDI-006), and the clean invoices (EDI-001, EDI-005, EDI-008). However, wi_skills produces a more complete and structured audit results file (11316 bytes vs 7502 bytes) with detailed validation metadata, per-invoice line-item breakdowns, cost recovery summaries, and richer AP system instructions. wo_skills fails the VC-U3-05 verifier check (EDI-003 not properly excluded from payment queue), indicating a structural defect. wo_skills calculates the EDI-002 overcharge correctly at $64 while wi_skills computes it as $60, giving wo_skills a slight edge on JE-U3-05. Neither run correctly implements the auto-approval threshold logic (JE-U3-04), which is the primary differentiator per the test design rationale. Overall, wi_skills is more effective due to its richer output structure, passing all verifier checks, and more detailed audit trail, while wo_skills has a critical verifier failure and less complete documentation.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills achieved a perfect score of 6/6 on all evaluation items, producing structurally complete, numerically accurate, and well-classified audit reports and payment instructions. The wi_skills run invoked the freight-audit-validator skill (steps 3-4), which provided a structured framework, but the wo_skills agent independently read all source files, performed the same rate calculations, accessorial cross-referencing, and duplicate detection, and produced outputs of comparable — arguably slightly richer — detail (e.g., more granular AP checklist, additional audit notes, per-charge breakdown in payment instructions). The two runs are effectively equivalent in quality for this scenario.

In wi_skills, the skill invocation via '/freight-audit-validator' failed (no such file or directory), and the fallback Python script produced empty/null records. utility_details.utility_summary: Neither wi_skills nor wo_skills produced a functionally correct audit. Both runs failed to parse the EDI files successfully, resulting in null data throughout all output records. The wi_skills run attempted to invoke the freight-audit-validator skill but it was not available as an executable, then fell back to a Python script that produced 8 'approved' entries all with $0.0 amounts and null fields — superficially passing some verifier checks (EDI-003 not approved because amount is 0) but containing no real audit logic. The wo_skills run used Node.js and produced more structured output with HOLD statuses and richer field schemas, but also failed to parse any EDI data. Neither run detected the duplicate (EDI-003), the accessorial violation (EDI-004), the weight discrepancy (EDI-006), applied threshold logic (EDI-007 vs EDI-002), or validated contract rates. Both are equally unusable for AP payment processing. Overall quality: both are effectively 0/7 on substantive evaluation items, making them equivalent in failure — though wo_skills shows marginally better structural intent (richer JSON schema, HOLD dispositions) while wi_skills passes one additional verifier check trivially. Neither is suitable for production use.

EDI-002, with a $64 overcharge above the threshold, is held for dispute. Pass: the threshold distinction is correctly applied to both invoices in opposite directions; Fail: both are treated the same way, or the threshold logic is not applied.

items.criterion: The payment queue file is structured with per-invoice approved amounts, disposition (approve/hold/dispute), and — for disputed invoices — the disputed amount and dispute basis, such that an AP system or operator can act on it without needing to re-examine the audit results. Pass: each invoice entry includes at minimum a disposition and an approved dollar amount, with dispute basis for non-approved items; Fail: the queue only lists invoice numbers without amounts or provides only narrative descriptions.
items.wi_reason: wi_skills payment queue includes per-invoice approved amounts, disposition (READY_FOR_PAYMENT / ON_HOLD / REJECTED), and for held/disputed items provides the dispute reason and disputed amount, making the queue AP-system-ready.
items.wo_reason: wo_skills payment queue includes per-invoice action (PAY/HOLD), approved amounts, billed amounts, disputed amounts, reason codes, and hold reasons for all non-approved items. The structure is marked 'ap_system_ready: true' and is actionable.

items.criterion: The three clean invoices (EDI-001, EDI-005, EDI-008) are correctly approved at their full billed amounts with no false discrepancy flags, demonstrating that the validation process does not generate spurious disputes. Pass: all three clean invoices appear in the payment queue as approved with amounts matching their billed totals; Fail: any clean invoice is flagged for a discrepancy that does not exist in the test data.
items.wi_reason: wi_skills correctly approves EDI-001 ($658.56), EDI-005 ($567.84), and EDI-008 ($780.64) at their full billed amounts with no false discrepancy flags and no spurious disputes.
items.wo_reason: wo_skills correctly approves INV-A-14001 ($658.56), INV-E-14005 ($567.84), and INV-H-14008 ($780.64) at full billed amounts with all checks passing and no false findings generated.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors detected in either wi_skills or wo_skills runs. Both completed successfully and produced valid JSON output files.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces higher quality output overall. Both runs correctly handle duplicate detection (JE-U3-01), accessorial verification (JE-U3-02), weight validation (JE-U3-03), rate validation (JE-U3-05), payment queue actionability (JE-U3-06), and clean invoice handling (JE-U3-07). The critical differentiator is JE-U3-04 (auto-approval threshold logic): wi_skills correctly short-pays EDI-007 at the contract amount of $784.00 while acknowledging the sub-threshold variance, whereas wo_skills approves EDI-007 at the full billed amount of $822.00 — paying the $38 overcharge instead of short-paying it. This is exactly the nuanced operational error the test was designed to detect. The wi_skills run, using the freight-audit-validator skill, applies the threshold correctly in both directions (short-pay under threshold, dispute over threshold), producing a more accurate and cost-saving payment queue.
